evals.prompt: I have a research paper at ./papers/ml-survey.pdf. Can you pull out the abstract and the first section so I can skim it without opening the file?
evals.expected_output: Probes the environment for an available backend, inspects the PDF to confirm it is text-readable, extracts text from the first few pages using the text command, and returns the abstract and first section to the user.
expectations: The skill probes for an available backend before attempting extraction
The skill inspects the PDF metadata or page count before extracting text
The skill uses the text command rather than render for a born-digital PDF
The extracted content includes the abstract and first section

evals.prompt: This invoice at ./scans/invoice-march.pdf seems to be a scan — copy-paste from the viewer gives nothing. Can you turn the pages into images so I can run OCR on them?
evals.expected_output: Probes the environment, inspects the PDF to confirm low or no text content, switches to render mode, converts the target pages to images, and saves them to an explicit output directory for downstream OCR processing.
expectations: The skill detects that text extraction is unlikely to work and switches to render
The skill renders pages as images using the appropriate backend
Rendered images are saved to an explicit, reported output directory
The skill does not falsely claim to have performed OCR

evals.prompt: I just downloaded a PDF called report-final.pdf and I have no idea what's in it or how big it is. Can you give me a quick overview before I decide whether to read it?
evals.expected_output: Probes the environment for available backends, runs inspect to report page count, metadata (title, author, creation date if present), and whether the PDF has bookmarks or an outline, then gives the user a concise summary to help them decide next steps.
expectations: The skill probes for available backends first
The skill runs inspect to gather metadata and structure info
The output includes page count and any available metadata
The skill reports whether bookmarks or an outline exist
The response is concise and helps the user decide whether to read deeper
